pr.title: Fix incorrect static in ittomato and mark as matching
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Corrected two Maxim Tomato helper declarations so they are no longer treated as local static symbols, exposed them through ittomato.h, and promoted ittomato.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The PR had no explanatory body or human review comments; the rationale is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/ittomato.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/ittomato.c").
Removed local static forward declarations for it_802841B4 and it_8028428C from src/melee/it/items/ittomato.c.
Added public prototypes for it_802841B4 and it_8028428C to src/melee/it/items/ittomato.h with their address comments.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 linked code at 30.66% (+0.03%, +1168 bytes) and linked data at 28.80% (+0.01%, +104 bytes).
smart_moves: Fixed symbol linkage rather than touching function bodies: a stray static prototype can force a later same-file definition to have internal linkage, which matters for matching and cross-translation-unit calls.
Moved only the two affected helper declarations into the header, while leaving unrelated local motion/callback prototypes static in the .c file.
Kept address comments on the newly exported prototypes, preserving the project convention for mapping unnamed symbols to DOL addresses.
Paired the linkage/header cleanup with the configure.py Matching promotion, indicating the object was verified after the static issue was resolved.
decomp_lessons: Matching can depend on C linkage and symbol visibility, not only instruction-level function body equivalence.
If an item helper must be visible outside its source file, avoid local static prototypes and declare it in the corresponding item header.
When removing an incorrect static declaration, audit whether the function should be exported through the header instead of relying on ad hoc externs.
Use the configure.py NonMatching-to-Matching flip as the final step after the object is known to link and match.
naming_conventions: Unnamed item functions keep address-derived names such as it_802841B4 and it_8028428C.
Address comments precede declarations, e.g. /* 2841B4 */ and /* 28428C */.
Named Maxim Tomato callbacks in the surrounding file follow itTomato_Logic9_* and itTomato_UnkMotionN_{Anim,Phys,Coll} patterns.
Item source/header pairing follows src/melee/it/items/ittomato.c and src/melee/it/items/ittomato.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Remove erroneous static declarations to restore external linkage where the original binary expects visible symbols.
Promote declarations to the subsystem header so callers share one prototype and the object can be treated as matching.
Keep unrelated internal callbacks static to minimize symbol-scope changes.
After linkage correction, mark the object Matching in configure.py so linked-code progress is captured.
review_feedback: No human review comments or PR body rationale were captured in the slice.
Only automated decomp.dev feedback was present, reporting GALE01 linked code/data gains and no listed changes beyond the metrics.
follow_up_queries: Find other item .c files where non-static definitions are preceded by static prototypes.
Audit item headers for externally called helpers that are still only locally declared.
Search for item objects still NonMatching because of symbol linkage or header declaration issues.
When promoting item objects to Matching, compare configure.py flips against decomp.dev linked-code/data reports.
